content.full_text:
T-8S 2/10
TO: ( ) JOHN L. ( ) BOB L. ( ) AL XX) JEANNE FROM: Kvasager, MA
( ) JOHN K. ( ) KATHY ( ) MARY ANN
DATE: 5/27/88
( ) LYNN ( ) JOHN M. ( ) CARLA
( ) JAY ( ) ROGER ( ) HELEN
( ) OTHER: _______________

XX) TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 82016, 82047.5

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [ ]

CALLER Jennie Foat

REPRESENTING CAL TASK

PHONE NUMBER: 415/752-9404

QUESTION: Is a committee "primarily formed" if it supports a bond measure
in the Nov. 8 election (all funds received are intended to
support this measure) and/makes an occasional contribution to
a couple of other measure committees formed for similar interests?
The measure is the result of a bill Senator Roberti sponsored. The
Senator is very interested in the measure and will be endorsing it.
ANSWER: The Senator will also attend fundraisers supporting the measure
and his name will appear on the measure committee's letterhead.

The measure committee is considered a primarily formed committee.
Based on the facts presented, the measure committee is not
controlled by Senator Roberti. I suggest she write the Commission
outlining the Senator's involvement.
